title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Control Systems - Definition, Examples (including
physiological control systems), System configurations, Design
considerations, Control system design process; Review of Laplace
Transforms, Transfer function of LTI system, Model of simple electrical
and mechanical systems; Concept of feedback, Reduction of Block
diagrams, Signal flow graph- Use of Mason's gain formula.

title: Module 2
content:
Time domain analysis- Standard test signals, Time response
specifications, First order system response, S-plane root location and
transient response, impulse and step response of second order systems,
performance and characteristics in the time domain, Steady state
response - Steady state error and static error coefficients.

title: Module 3
content:
Frequency domain analysis - Frequency response techniques -
frequency response specifications, bode plots, gain margin and phase
margin via Bode plots; Routh - Hurwitz criterion, Root Locus
techniques, Concepts of relative stability. Overview of compensators
for design improvement.

title: Module 4
content:
State space models - Introduction to the state variable concept, State
model of mechanical and electrical systems. Various forms of
representing state space systems; Solution of continuous time state
equations - (Homogeneous case only), State transition matrix -
properties, Controllability, Observability.
